schools.summary: The LORD speaking to Moses concerning the sin offering: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — the LORD speaking to Moses, now to give the law of the sin offering (the trespass against the commandments through ignorance, vv.2-35, the speaking, God's command, opening the new law of the sin offering, the occasion, after the laws of the burnt, meal, and peace offerings, chs. 1-3, now the offering for sin); the LORD speaking (the new law given, the sin offering distinct from the previous offerings, which were sweet savours, the sin offering for the expiation of guilt) — the divine authority (the law from God, the new offering, the sin offering, not a sweet savour but for sin, the first of the expiatory offerings); the LORD spake unto Moses — the law of the sin offering introduced (the providence, God revealing the way of atonement for sin, the offering for the guilt of transgression) — Henry, Gill, Calvin, Wesley.

themes: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying
the LORD speaking to Moses concerning the sin offering
God giving the law of the sin offering, the first of the expiatory offerings (vv.2-35)
revealing the way of atonement for the guilt of transgression

schools.summary: Sin through ignorance against God's commandments: 'speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a soul shall sin through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and shall do against any of them' — the ignorance — 'if a soul shall sin through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done': the sin offering for sins committed through ignorance (unwittingly, inadvertently, in error, not presumptuously or with a high hand, Numbers 15:30, the witnesses noting the significance — the sins of ignorance, sins committed unwittingly, through inadvertence, weakness, or want of knowledge, yet still sins, still needing atonement, the lesson — the sinfulness of sin even unwitting, sin is sin against God's law, whether known or not, the conscience defiled, the guilt real, the need of atonement, 'who can understand his errors? cleanse thou me from secret faults,' Psalm 19:12, the holiness of God's law, the smallest, most unwitting breach still a sin requiring atonement, the contrast with presumptuous sin, the high-handed sin, the defiant, for which no sacrifice was appointed, Numbers 15:30-31; Hebrews 10:26, the figure — the need of atonement even for sins of ignorance, fulfilled in Christ who cleanses from all sin, even the secret and unknown, 1 John 1:7-9); the doing of what ought not to be done (the breach of a negative commandment, the transgression of God's law) — the guilt (the commandment broken, the sin real, the providence, the sin offering for the breach, even unwitting, the atonement provided); sin through ignorance — the unwitting transgression (the providence, the sin of ignorance needing atonement, the holiness of the law, the cleansing in Christ) — Wesley, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: What does the sin offering for sins of ignorance teach?

sides.position: The sins of ignorance, committed unwittingly yet still sins needing atonement (Psalm 19:12) - the holiness of God's law (the smallest unwitting breach a real sin), in contrast with presumptuous sin for which no sacrifice was given (Numbers 15:30-31; Hebrews 10:26), the need of atonement even for secret sins, fulfilled in Christ who cleanses from all sin (1 John 1:7-9)

themes: If a soul shall sin through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and shall do against any of them
sin through ignorance against God's commandments (the crux of the sinfulness of unwitting sin)
the sins of ignorance still real sins needing atonement, the holiness of the law (Psalm 19:12)
in contrast with presumptuous sin, the cleansing in Christ from all sin (Numbers 15:30-31; 1 John 1:7-9)

schools.summary: The sin offering for the anointed priest: 'if the priest that is anointed do sin according to the sin of the people; then let him bring for his sin, which he hath sinned, a young bullock without blemish unto the LORD for a sin offering' — if the anointed (high) priest sinned, bringing guilt on the people, he was to bring a young bullock (the costliest victim) for a sin offering (the anointed priest, the high priest, the most responsible, his sin the most serious, the sin 'according to the sin of the people,' his sin bringing guilt on the people, or as the people would sin, the public consequence of the priest's sin, the bullock, the costliest victim, fitting the gravity of the priest's sin); the priest's sin — the high priest's sin the most grave, bringing guilt on the people (the witnesses noting the lesson — the sin of the leader most serious, the higher the office, the greater the sin and its consequence, the priest's sin defiling the sanctuary and the people, the public guilt, the contrast with Christ, the Aaronic high priest himself a sinner, needing to offer for his own sin, Hebrews 5:3; 7:27, 'who needeth not daily... to offer up sacrifice, first for his own sins,' but Christ the sinless High Priest, needing no offering for himself, holy, harmless, undefiled, Hebrews 7:26-27, the costliest victim, the bullock, the gravity of the sin); the bullock without blemish (the costliest, unblemished victim, the sin offering) — the figure (the unblemished victim, Christ the lamb without blemish, 1 Peter 1:19, the providence, the priest's sin offering, the figure of the need of atonement and of the sinless Christ) — Calvin, Wesley, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: What does the anointed priest's sin offering teach?

sides.position: The high priest's sin the most grave, bringing guilt on the people, requiring the costliest victim - a witness to the Aaronic priest's own sinfulness (needing to offer for himself, Hebrews 5:3; 7:27), in contrast with Christ the sinless High Priest who needed no offering for himself (Hebrews 7:26-27), the unblemished victim a figure of Christ the Lamb without blemish (1 Peter 1:19)

themes: If the priest that is anointed do sin according to the sin of the people; then let him bring... a young bullock without blemish unto the LORD for a sin offering
the sin offering for the anointed priest
the high priest's sin the most grave, bringing guilt on the people, the costliest victim (Hebrews 5:3; 7:27)
in contrast with Christ the sinless High Priest, the unblemished victim (Hebrews 7:26-27; 1 Peter 1:19)

schools.summary: The laying on of the hand and the killing of the victim: 'and he shall bring the bullock unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD; and shall lay his hand upon the bullock's head, and kill the bullock before the LORD' — the imposition — 'he shall... lay his hand upon the bullock's head': the offerer laying his hand on the head of the victim before it was killed (the witnesses noting the significance — the laying on of the hand the act of identification and substitution, the offerer putting himself, as it were, on the victim, transferring his sin and guilt to it, the victim standing in his place, bearing his sin and dying in his stead, the lesson — the great principle of substitution, the innocent victim bearing the guilt of the sinner, dying in his place, the sin transferred by the laying on of the hand, the figure of the imputation of our sin to Christ, the figure — Christ the true sin offering, on whom our sin was laid, who bore our sins in his own body, Isaiah 53:6, 'the LORD hath laid on him the iniquity of us all,' 1 Peter 2:24, 'who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree,' 2 Corinthians 5:21, 'he hath made him to be sin for us,' the confession, the laying on of the hand often with confession of sin over the victim, Leviticus 16:21); the killing of the victim (the death of the substitute, the life given for the sinner, the blood shed) — the death (the wages of sin, the victim dying in the sinner's place, 'without shedding of blood is no remission,' Hebrews 9:22, the figure, the death of Christ the substitute for sinners); the hand laid on the head, the victim killed — the transfer of guilt and the death of the substitute (the providence, the substitution figured, the sin laid on the victim, fulfilled in Christ) — Wesley, JFB, Gill.

debates.question: What does the laying on of the hand signify?

sides.position: The act of identification and substitution - the sin transferred to the victim, which bears it and dies in the sinner's place, the great principle of substitution; a figure of the imputation of our sin to Christ on whom the LORD laid our iniquity (Isaiah 53:6; 1 Peter 2:24; 2 Corinthians 5:21), and the death of the substitute (Hebrews 9:22)

themes: And he shall bring the bullock unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD; and shall lay his hand upon the bullock's head, and kill the bullock before the LORD
the laying on of the hand and the killing of the victim (the crux of substitution)
the sin transferred to the substitute, which dies in the sinner's place
the figure of our sin laid on Christ who bore it in his own body (Isaiah 53:6; 1 Peter 2:24; 2 Corinthians 5:21)

schools.summary: The priest taking the blood into the tabernacle: 'and the priest that is anointed shall take of the bullock's blood, and bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation' — the anointed priest taking the blood of the slain victim and bringing it into the holy place (the blood, the life of the victim, the means of atonement, 'the life of the flesh is in the blood... it is the blood that maketh an atonement for the soul,' Leviticus 17:11, the bringing in, the blood brought into the sanctuary, nearer to God, the gravity of the priest's sin requiring the blood within, the figure, the blood the means of atonement, the blood of Christ brought into the true sanctuary, Hebrews 9:12); the blood taken (the life of the victim, the atonement, Leviticus 17:11) — the figure (the blood of Christ, the means of atonement, Hebrews 9:14); 'bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation' (the blood brought into the holy place, the gravity of the sin, the blood nearer to God, the figure, the blood brought into the sanctuary, Christ entering the holy place by his own blood, Hebrews 9:12, the providence, the blood brought in, the atonement made within) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill, Calvin, Ellicott, Wesley.

themes: And the priest that is anointed shall take of the bullock's blood, and bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation
the priest taking the blood into the tabernacle
the blood the means of atonement, the life of the victim (Leviticus 17:11)
the figure of Christ entering the holy place by his own blood (Hebrews 9:12, 14)

schools.summary: The sevenfold sprinkling of the blood before the veil: 'and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle of the blood seven times before the LORD, before the vail of the sanctuary' — the priest sprinkling the blood seven times before the veil (the veil dividing the holy from the most holy, the sprinkling, the application of the blood, the atonement applied, the seven times, the perfect, complete number, the full atonement, the veil, the blood sprinkled before the veil, toward the most holy place, the presence of God, the figure, the blood of sprinkling, the perfect atonement of Christ applied, Hebrews 12:24; 10:22); the sevenfold sprinkling (the perfect, complete atonement, the number of perfection) — the figure (the complete, perfect atonement of Christ, the blood of sprinkling, Hebrews 12:24); 'before the vail of the sanctuary' (the blood sprinkled toward the most holy place, the presence of God, the atonement before the throne, the figure, the blood presented before God, the access by the blood, Hebrews 10:19-22, the providence, the blood sprinkled before the veil, the atonement applied before God) — Clarke, Gill, Ellicott, Wesley.

themes: And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle of the blood seven times before the LORD, before the vail of the sanctuary
the sevenfold sprinkling of the blood before the veil
the perfect, complete atonement applied (the seven times)
the figure of the blood of sprinkling of Christ, the access by the blood (Hebrews 12:24; 10:19-22)

schools.summary: The blood put on the horns of the golden altar and poured at the base: 'and the priest shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the LORD, which is in the tabernacle of the congregation; and shall pour all the blood of the bullock at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which is at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation' — some of the blood put on the horns of the golden altar of incense (in the holy place, the gravity of the priest's sin requiring the blood there), and the rest poured at the base of the brazen altar (the horns, the points of the altar, the blood applied to the place of prayer, the pouring out, the rest of the blood poured at the base, the full giving of the life, the figure, the blood applied to the altar of prayer, the atonement reaching even the place of intercession, the blood of Christ); the blood on the horns of the golden altar (the altar of prayer, the gravity of the priest's sin reaching even there, Exodus 30:10) — the figure (the atonement reaching the place of prayer, the blood cleansing even our worship); 'pour all the blood of the bullock at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering' (the rest of the blood poured at the base, the full giving of the life, the blood not wasted, the figure, the life wholly poured out, Christ pouring out his soul unto death, Isaiah 53:12, the providence, the blood applied and poured out, the atonement complete) — Ellicott, Gill, Wesley.

themes: And the priest shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the LORD... and shall pour all the blood of the bullock at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering
the blood put on the horns of the golden altar and poured at the base
the atonement reaching even the place of prayer (Exodus 30:10)
the life wholly poured out, the figure of Christ pouring out his soul unto death (Isaiah 53:12)

schools.summary: The taking of the fat of the sin offering: 'and he shall take off from it all the fat of the bullock for the sin offering; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards' — all the fat of the bullock (the fat covering and upon the inwards) taken off, to be burned on the altar (the fat, the best, richest part, the choicest, reserved for God, Leviticus 3:16, the inwards, the inner parts, the seat of the affections, the figure, the fat, the best given to God, the inward devotion, the choicest offered); the fat taken off (the best part, burned to God, Leviticus 3:16) — the figure (the best, the inward, given to God, the choicest of the offering); 'the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards' (the fat of the inner parts, the seat of the affections, the inward, the figure, the inward devotion, the heart and affections given to God, the best inwardly offered, the providence, the fat reserved for God, the best of the sin offering burned) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And he shall take off from it all the fat of the bullock for the sin offering; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards
the taking of the fat of the sin offering
the choicest part reserved for God (Leviticus 3:16)
the figure of the inward devotion and the best offered

schools.summary: The taking of the kidneys and the caul: 'and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away' — the kidneys with their fat and the caul (the membrane) above the liver taken away, to be burned (the kidneys, the inner organs, with their fat, the choicest inward parts, Leviticus 3:4, the caul, the membrane over the liver, the figure, the inward parts given to God, the seat of the affections and the inward man, the best of the inward offered); the kidneys and their fat (the choicest inward parts, Leviticus 3:4) — the figure (the inward parts, the seat of the deepest feeling, given to God); 'the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away' (the caul and kidneys taken away, the inward parts burned, Leviticus 3:4, the figure, the inward man, the affections, wholly given to God, the providence, the inward parts reserved for God, the best burned) — Keil and Delitzsch, Ellicott.

themes: And the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away
the taking of the kidneys and the caul
the inward parts given to God, the seat of the affections (Leviticus 3:4)
the figure of the inner man wholly offered

schools.summary: The burning of the fat on the altar, as in the peace offering: 'as it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall burn them upon the altar of the burnt offering' — the fat and inward parts taken off as in the peace offering (Leviticus 3:3-5), and burned on the brazen altar (the burning, the fat burned to God, the sweet savour, even in the sin offering, the likeness, as in the peace offering, the same parts burned, the figure, the best given to God, even in the offering for sin, the inward devotion of Christ, the sweet savour of his offering, Ephesians 5:2); the fat taken as in the peace offering (the same parts, Leviticus 3:3-5) — the likeness (the fat burned to God in both); 'the priest shall burn them upon the altar of the burnt offering' (the fat burned on the altar, the best given to God, the figure, the sweet savour, the inward devotion of Christ even in bearing sin, Ephesians 5:2, the providence, the fat burned, the best given to God even in the sin offering) — JFB.

themes: As it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall burn them upon the altar of the burnt offering
the burning of the fat on the altar, as in the peace offering
the best given to God even in the sin offering (Leviticus 3:3-5)
the figure of the sweet savour of Christ's inward devotion (Ephesians 5:2)

schools.summary: The whole body of the bullock to be carried out: 'and the skin of the bullock, and all his flesh, with his head, and with his legs, and his inwards, and his dung' — the whole body of the bullock (skin, flesh, head, legs, inwards, dung — everything but the fat burned on the altar) to be carried out (the whole body, all the rest of the victim, not eaten by the priest in this case, but wholly removed, the figure, the whole body of the sin offering for the priest carried out and burned without the camp, vv.12, the distinction, this sin offering, for the priest, wholly burned without, not eaten, unlike the offerings whose blood was not brought into the holy place); the whole body (the skin, flesh, and all, to be carried out, vv.12) — the removal (the whole body removed, burned without); 'with his head, and with his legs, and his inwards, and his dung' (the whole body, even the dung, all carried out, nothing of the body remaining on the altar, the figure, the whole sin-bearing victim removed, burned without the camp, vv.12; Hebrews 13:11, the providence, the body wholly carried out, the figure of Christ suffering without the gate) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And the skin of the bullock, and all his flesh, with his head, and with his legs, and his inwards, and his dung
the whole body of the bullock to be carried out
the whole sin-bearing victim removed, nothing remaining on the altar
to be burned without the camp, the figure of Christ suffering without the gate (vv.12; Hebrews 13:11)

schools.summary: The burning of the bullock without the camp: 'even the whole bullock shall he carry forth without the camp unto a clean place, where the ashes are poured out, and burn him on the wood with fire: where the ashes are poured out shall he be burnt' — the place — 'the whole bullock shall he carry forth without the camp unto a clean place... and burn him': the body of the sin offering for the priest (and for the congregation, vv.21) carried outside the camp to a clean place and wholly burned (the witnesses noting the significance — the body burned without the camp, not on the altar, not eaten, but consumed outside the camp, the place of uncleanness and rejection, the lesson — the figure expounded fully in Hebrews — the bodies of the sin offerings whose blood was brought into the sanctuary were burned without the camp, Hebrews 13:11, 'wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate,' Hebrews 13:12, Christ the true sin offering, suffering outside the gate of Jerusalem, crucified without the city, bearing the reproach and rejection, made sin and a curse for us, cast out as unclean that we might be sanctified, the call to follow, Hebrews 13:13, 'let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach,' the figure of Christ's rejection and the believer's fellowship in his reproach); the clean place (the appointed place outside the camp, where the ashes were poured) — the disposal (the body wholly consumed, the sin-bearing victim removed, the figure, the place of rejection, yet a clean place, Christ cast out yet holy); the body burned without the camp — the rejection of the sin-bearer (the providence, the figure of Christ suffering without the gate, Hebrews 13:11-13) — Wesley, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: What does the burning of the sin offering without the camp signify?

sides.position: The sin offering's body burned outside the camp (Hebrews 13:11) - the figure of Christ who 'suffered without the gate' (Hebrews 13:12), bearing reproach and rejection, made sin for us, cast out as unclean that we might be sanctified, with the call to go forth to him bearing his reproach (Hebrews 13:13)

themes: Even the whole bullock shall he carry forth without the camp unto a clean place, where the ashes are poured out, and burn him on the wood with fire
the burning of the bullock without the camp (the crux of Christ suffering without the gate)
the sin offering burned outside the camp (Hebrews 13:11)
the figure of Christ who suffered without the gate, bearing our reproach (Hebrews 13:12-13)

schools.summary: The sin of the whole congregation through ignorance: 'and if the whole congregation of Israel sin through ignorance, and the thing be hid from the eyes of the assembly, and they have done somewhat against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which should not be done, and are guilty' — if the whole congregation sinned through ignorance (the sin hidden from them, not known), they were guilty and needed atonement (the corporate sin, the whole nation sinning, the public, national guilt, the hidden, the sin not known, done in ignorance, yet still guilt, the guilt, the congregation guilty, needing the sin offering); the sin of the whole congregation — the corporate, national sin (the whole assembly guilty, even unwittingly, the witnesses noting the lesson — national and corporate guilt, a whole people may sin together, may be guilty of common, hidden sins, needing atonement, the solidarity of the nation in sin, the need of atonement for the whole body, the figure, the corporate guilt of the people, the need of the one sacrifice for the whole, Christ the propitiation for the sins of the whole people, John 11:50-51; 1 John 2:2); the congregation guilty (the guilt real, even in ignorance) — the providence (the corporate guilt, the sin offering for the whole congregation, vv.14-21, the figure, the atonement for the whole people) — Henry, Calvin, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the sin of the whole congregation teach?

sides.position: The corporate, national guilt - a whole people sinning together, even unwittingly, in common hidden sins, the solidarity of the nation in sin, the need of atonement for the whole body; a figure of the one sacrifice of Christ the propitiation for the sins of the whole people (John 11:50-51; 1 John 2:2)

themes: And if the whole congregation of Israel sin through ignorance, and the thing be hid from the eyes of the assembly... and are guilty
the sin of the whole congregation through ignorance
the corporate, national guilt, the solidarity of the nation in sin
a figure of Christ the propitiation for the sins of the whole people (John 11:50-51; 1 John 2:2)

schools.summary: The congregation bringing a bullock when the sin is known: 'when the sin, which they have sinned against it, is known, then the congregation shall offer a young bullock for the sin, and bring him before the tabernacle of the congregation' — when the congregation's sin became known, they were to offer a young bullock (the same costly victim as for the priest) for a sin offering (the making known, the sin discovered, brought to light, the conscience awakened, the bullock, the costliest victim, the gravity of the corporate sin, as for the priest, vv.3, the offering, the congregation bringing the sacrifice for the whole); the sin becoming known (the conscience awakened, the guilt acknowledged) — the conviction (the sin discovered, the need of atonement felt); 'the congregation shall offer a young bullock for the sin' (the bullock, the costliest victim, as for the priest, the gravity of the national sin, the figure, the one sacrifice for the whole congregation, the atonement for the people, the providence, the congregation's sin offering, the figure of Christ for the whole people) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill, Wesley.

themes: When the sin, which they have sinned against it, is known, then the congregation shall offer a young bullock for the sin, and bring him before the tabernacle of the congregation
the congregation bringing a bullock when the sin is known
the costliest victim for the corporate sin, as for the priest (vv.3)
the figure of the one sacrifice of Christ for the whole people

schools.summary: The elders laying their hands on the victim for the congregation: 'and the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the LORD: and the bullock shall be killed before the LORD' — the elders (the representatives of the whole congregation) laying their hands on the head of the victim, on behalf of all the people, and it being killed (the elders, the representatives, acting for the whole congregation, the laying on of hands, the act of identification and substitution, the congregation's sin transferred to the victim, as in vv.4, the killing, the death of the substitute for the people, the figure, the representatives confessing the corporate sin, the sin laid on the victim, fulfilled in Christ bearing the sin of the people); the elders laying on hands for the whole congregation (the representatives, the corporate sin transferred, vv.4) — the substitution (the congregation's sin laid on the victim by the elders' hands); 'the bullock shall be killed before the LORD' (the death of the substitute, for the whole congregation, the figure, the death of Christ for the people, the substitute slain, the providence, the victim killed for the congregation, the figure of Christ for the people) — Ellicott, Gill, Wesley.

themes: And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the LORD: and the bullock shall be killed before the LORD
the elders laying their hands on the victim for the congregation
the corporate sin transferred to the victim by the representatives (vv.4)
the figure of Christ bearing the sin of the people

schools.summary: The priest bringing the congregation's blood into the tabernacle: 'and the priest that is anointed shall bring of the bullock's blood to the tabernacle of the congregation' — the anointed priest bringing the blood of the congregation's sin offering into the holy place, as with his own (vv.5, the blood, the means of atonement for the congregation, the bringing in, the blood brought into the sanctuary, the gravity of the corporate sin, as with the priest's, the figure, the blood the means of atonement, the blood of Christ for the people, Hebrews 9:12); the priest bringing the blood (the atonement for the congregation, as for himself, vv.5) — the figure (the blood of Christ for the people); 'to the tabernacle of the congregation' (the blood brought into the holy place, the gravity of the corporate sin, the same ritual as for the priest, the figure, the blood brought into the sanctuary, Christ entering by his own blood, Hebrews 9:12, the providence, the congregation's blood brought in, the atonement made within) — JFB, Gill.

themes: And the priest that is anointed shall bring of the bullock's blood to the tabernacle of the congregation
the priest bringing the congregation's blood into the tabernacle
the blood the means of atonement for the congregation, as for the priest (vv.5)
the figure of Christ entering the holy place by his own blood for the people (Hebrews 9:12)

schools.summary: The sevenfold sprinkling of the congregation's blood before the veil: 'and the priest shall dip his finger in some of the blood, and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD, even before the vail' — the priest sprinkling the congregation's blood seven times before the veil, as with the priest's own offering (vv.6, the sprinkling, the application of the blood, the atonement applied for the congregation, the seven times, the perfect, complete atonement, the veil, the blood sprinkled toward the most holy place, before God, the figure, the perfect atonement of Christ applied for the people, the blood of sprinkling, Hebrews 12:24); the sevenfold sprinkling (the complete atonement, vv.6) — the figure (the perfect atonement of Christ applied); 'even before the vail' (the blood sprinkled toward the most holy place, before God, the atonement presented before the throne, the figure, the access by the blood, Hebrews 10:19-22, the providence, the blood applied before God for the congregation) — Wesley.

themes: And the priest shall dip his finger in some of the blood, and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD, even before the vail
the sevenfold sprinkling of the congregation's blood before the veil
the perfect, complete atonement applied for the people (vv.6)
the figure of the blood of sprinkling of Christ (Hebrews 12:24; 10:19-22)

schools.summary: The atonement made and the congregation forgiven: 'and he shall do with the bullock as he did with the bullock for a sin offering, so shall he do with this: and the priest shall make an atonement for them, and it shall be forgiven them' — the priest making atonement for the congregation by the sin offering (done as the priest's own, vv.3-12), and their sin being forgiven (the atonement, the covering of sin by the blood, the priest making atonement, the forgiveness, the sin forgiven, the great end and promise of the sin offering, 'it shall be forgiven them,' the figure, the atonement by the blood, the forgiveness of sins through the one sacrifice of Christ, Hebrews 9:22; 10:18; Ephesians 1:7, 'in whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins'); the priest making atonement (the covering of sin by the blood, the propitiation) — the figure (Christ our great High Priest making atonement, the one sacrifice, Hebrews 9:26; 10:12); 'it shall be forgiven them' (the sin forgiven, the assured promise, the end of the sin offering, the pardon granted, the figure, the forgiveness of sins through Christ's atonement, the assured pardon, 1 John 1:7-9; Acts 13:38-39, the providence, the atonement made, the forgiveness granted, the great gift of the sin offering) — Wesley.

themes: And the priest shall make an atonement for them, and it shall be forgiven them
the atonement made and the congregation forgiven
the atonement by the blood and the assured forgiveness, the great end of the sin offering
a figure of the forgiveness of sins through the one sacrifice of Christ (Hebrews 9:22; 10:18; Ephesians 1:7)

schools.summary: The congregation's bullock burned without the camp: 'and he shall carry forth the bullock without the camp, and burn him as he burned the first bullock: it is a sin offering for the congregation' — the body of the congregation's sin offering carried out and burned without the camp, as the priest's bullock (vv.12, the burning without the camp, the body removed and burned outside, the same ritual as the priest's, the figure, as the priest's, the figure of Christ suffering without the gate, Hebrews 13:11-13, the sin offering for the congregation, the atonement for the whole people); the body burned without the camp as the first bullock (vv.12) — the figure (Christ suffering without the gate, Hebrews 13:11-13); 'it is a sin offering for the congregation' (the sin offering for the whole people, the atonement for the congregation, the figure, the one sacrifice for the whole people, Christ for the nation, John 11:51-52, the providence, the congregation's sin offering, the figure of Christ for the people) — Keil and Delitzsch.

themes: And he shall carry forth the bullock without the camp, and burn him as he burned the first bullock: it is a sin offering for the congregation
the congregation's bullock burned without the camp
the body burned outside the camp as the priest's (vv.12)
the figure of Christ suffering without the gate for the whole people (Hebrews 13:11-13; John 11:51-52)

schools.summary: The sin offering for a ruler: 'when a ruler hath sinned, and done somewhat through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD his God concerning things which should not be done, and is guilty' — when a ruler (a prince, the head of a tribe, Numbers 3:24) sinned through ignorance, he was guilty and needed atonement (the ruler, the prince, a man of rank and authority, the sin through ignorance, the unwitting transgression, as the others, the guilt, the ruler guilty, needing the sin offering, his goat, vv.23); the 'when' (not 'if') — 'when a ruler hath sinned': the text saying 'when,' almost assuming that rulers will sin (the great and powerful as liable to sin as others, even more exposed to temptation, the witnesses noting the lesson — rulers and the great are sinners too, no rank exempt from sin, the prince needing atonement as the common man, the higher placed often the more tempted, the warning to those in authority, the figure, all, high and low, sinners needing atonement, Romans 3:23, the one Saviour for all ranks); 'the LORD his God' (the ruler still under God, accountable to him, however high) — the accountability (the ruler subject to God's law, no rank above it, the providence, the ruler's sin offering, the figure of all ranks needing atonement) — Henry, Calvin, JFB, Gill.

debates.question: What does the sin offering for a ruler teach?

sides.position: The great and powerful as liable to sin as others ('when,' not 'if'), no rank exempt, often the more tempted, still accountable to 'the LORD his God' - a figure of all ranks, high and low, as sinners needing atonement (Romans 3:23) and the one Saviour for all

themes: When a ruler hath sinned, and done somewhat through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD his God concerning things which should not be done, and is guilty
the sin offering for a ruler
the great as liable to sin as others ('when,' not 'if'), no rank exempt, accountable to God
a figure of all ranks needing atonement and the one Saviour for all (Romans 3:23)

schools.summary: The ruler bringing a male goat when his sin is known: 'or if his sin, wherein he hath sinned, come to his knowledge; he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a male without blemish' — when the ruler's sin came to his knowledge, he bringing a male kid of the goats (without blemish) for a sin offering (the making known, the sin brought to his knowledge, the conscience awakened, the male goat, a lesser victim than the bullock for the priest or congregation, fitting the lesser public consequence of a ruler's private sin, the without blemish, the unblemished victim, the figure of Christ, 1 Peter 1:19); the sin coming to his knowledge (the conscience awakened, the guilt acknowledged) — the conviction (the sin discovered, the need of atonement felt); 'a kid of the goats, a male without blemish' (the male goat, the gradation of victims, the ruler's offering, less than the bullock, the figure, the unblemished victim, Christ without blemish, 1 Peter 1:19, the providence, the ruler's sin offering, the figure of the spotless Christ) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: Or if his sin, wherein he hath sinned, come to his knowledge; he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a male without blemish
the ruler bringing a male goat when his sin is known
the male goat, a lesser victim than the bullock, fitting the ruler's offering
the unblemished victim, the figure of the spotless Christ (1 Peter 1:19)

schools.summary: The ruler laying his hand on the goat and killing it: 'and he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD: it is a sin offering' — the ruler laying his hand on the goat's head and killing it at the place of the burnt offering (the laying on of the hand, the identification and substitution, the sin transferred to the victim, as vv.4, the killing, the death of the substitute, the place, where the burnt offering was killed, the appointed place of sacrifice, the figure, the sin laid on the substitute, the death in the sinner's place, fulfilled in Christ, Isaiah 53:6); the hand laid on the goat (the sin transferred, the substitution, vv.4) — the figure (the sin laid on Christ, Isaiah 53:6); 'kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD: it is a sin offering' (the death of the substitute at the place of sacrifice, the sin offering, the figure, the death of Christ the substitute, Hebrews 9:22, the providence, the victim slain for the ruler, the figure of Christ) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill, Wesley.

themes: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD: it is a sin offering
the ruler laying his hand on the goat and killing it
the sin transferred to the substitute, which dies in the sinner's place (vv.4)
the figure of Christ bearing the sin (Isaiah 53:6; Hebrews 9:22)

schools.summary: The blood of the ruler's offering on the brazen altar: 'and the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out his blood at the bottom of the altar of burnt offering' — the priest putting the blood on the horns of the brazen altar (not the golden altar within, as for the priest and congregation), and pouring the rest at its base (the blood on the horns, the atonement applied to the altar of sacrifice in the court, the difference, the blood not brought into the holy place, the ruler's sin less in public consequence than the priest's or congregation's, the pouring out, the rest of the blood poured at the base, the full giving of the life, the figure, the blood the means of atonement, the life poured out, Christ); the blood on the horns of the brazen altar (the altar of sacrifice in the court, not the golden altar within, vv.7) — the difference (the lesser ritual, the ruler's sin not requiring the blood within); 'pour out his blood at the bottom of the altar of burnt offering' (the rest of the blood poured at the base, the full giving of the life, the figure, the life poured out, Christ pouring out his soul, Isaiah 53:12, the providence, the blood applied and poured out, the atonement for the ruler) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out his blood at the bottom of the altar of burnt offering
the blood of the ruler's offering on the brazen altar
the atonement applied at the altar of sacrifice, the blood not brought within (vv.7)
the life poured out, the figure of Christ pouring out his soul (Isaiah 53:12)

schools.summary: The fat burned and the ruler forgiven: 'and he shall burn all his fat upon the altar, as the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall make an atonement for him as concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him' — the fat burned on the altar (as in the peace offering), and the priest making atonement for the ruler, and his sin being forgiven (the fat burned, the best given to God, the sweet savour, even in the sin offering, the atonement, the covering of sin by the blood, the forgiveness, the sin forgiven, the great promise, 'it shall be forgiven him,' the figure, the atonement and forgiveness through Christ, Hebrews 9:22; Ephesians 1:7); the fat burned (the best to God, as the peace offering, Leviticus 3:16) — the figure (the inward devotion, the sweet savour); 'the priest shall make an atonement for him as concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him' (the atonement made and the forgiveness granted, the assured pardon, the figure, the forgiveness of sins through Christ's atonement, 1 John 1:7-9; Ephesians 1:7, the providence, the atonement made, the ruler forgiven) — Gill, Wesley.

themes: And he shall burn all his fat upon the altar... and the priest shall make an atonement for him as concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him
the fat burned and the ruler forgiven
the best given to God and the atonement by the blood (Leviticus 3:16; Hebrews 9:22)
the assured forgiveness of sins through Christ (Ephesians 1:7; 1 John 1:7-9)

schools.summary: The sin offering for the common people: 'and if any one of the common people sin through ignorance, while he doeth somewhat against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and be guilty' — if any common person (one of the ordinary people, 'the people of the land') sinned through ignorance, he was guilty and needed atonement (the common people, the ordinary individual, the humblest, the sin through ignorance, the unwitting transgression, as the others, the guilt, the common man guilty, needing the sin offering, his goat or lamb, vv.28, 32); the gradation completed — the sin offering provided for every rank: the priest, the congregation, the ruler, and now the common people (the witnesses noting the lesson — the provision of atonement for all, high and low, none too humble for the sin offering, the way of atonement open to every individual, the poorest and humblest as much as the priest and prince, the equal need and equal provision, all are sinners, Romans 3:23, and the way of atonement is for all, 'whosoever,' John 3:16, the one sacrifice of Christ sufficient and open for every individual, 1 John 2:2, the common man's access to atonement as real as the priest's); the common man guilty (the guilt real, the need of atonement) — the providence (the sin offering for the individual, vv.28-35, the way of atonement for every soul) — Henry, Gill.

debates.question: What does the sin offering for the common people teach?

sides.position: The provision of atonement for every rank completed (priest, congregation, ruler, and now the humblest individual) - the way of atonement open to all, high and low, none too humble (all sinners, Romans 3:23, and the one sacrifice of Christ for every individual, 'whosoever,' John 3:16; 1 John 2:2)

themes: And if any one of the common people sin through ignorance, while he doeth somewhat against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and be guilty
the sin offering for the common people
the provision of atonement for every rank completed, the way open to all, none too humble
all sinners, the one sacrifice of Christ for every individual (Romans 3:23; John 3:16; 1 John 2:2)

schools.summary: The common man bringing a female goat when his sin is known: 'or if his sin, which he hath sinned, come to his knowledge: then he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a female without blemish, for his sin which he hath sinned' — when the common man's sin came to his knowledge, he bringing a female kid of the goats (without blemish) for a sin offering (the making known, the sin discovered, the conscience awakened, the female goat, a lesser victim than the ruler's male goat, fitting the humbler offerer, the gradation of victims, the without blemish, the unblemished victim, the figure of Christ, 1 Peter 1:19); the sin coming to his knowledge (the conscience awakened) — the conviction (the sin discovered, the atonement sought); 'a kid of the goats, a female without blemish' (the female goat, the gradation, the humbler offerer's victim, the figure, the unblemished victim, Christ without blemish, 1 Peter 1:19, the providence, the common man's sin offering, the figure of the spotless Christ) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill, Wesley.

themes: Or if his sin, which he hath sinned, come to his knowledge: then he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a female without blemish, for his sin which he hath sinned
the common man bringing a female goat when his sin is known
the female goat, a lesser victim fitting the humbler offerer, the gradation of victims
the unblemished victim, the figure of the spotless Christ (1 Peter 1:19)

schools.summary: The common man laying his hand on the victim and slaying it: 'and he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering' — the common man laying his hand on the victim's head and slaying it at the place of sacrifice (the laying on of the hand, the identification and substitution, the sin transferred, as vv.4, the slaying, the death of the substitute, the place, the place of the burnt offering, the appointed place of sacrifice, the figure, the sin laid on the substitute, the death in the sinner's place, fulfilled in Christ, Isaiah 53:6); the hand laid on the victim (the sin transferred, vv.4) — the figure (the sin laid on Christ, Isaiah 53:6); 'slay the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering' (the death of the substitute at the place of sacrifice, the figure, the death of Christ the substitute, Hebrews 9:22, the providence, the victim slain for the common man, the figure of Christ) — Gill.

themes: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering
the common man laying his hand on the victim and slaying it
the sin transferred to the substitute, which dies in the sinner's place (vv.4)
the figure of Christ bearing the sin (Isaiah 53:6; Hebrews 9:22)

schools.summary: The blood of the common man's offering on the altar: 'and the priest shall take of the blood thereof with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar' — the priest putting the blood on the horns of the brazen altar and pouring the rest at its base, as for the ruler (vv.25, the blood on the horns, the atonement applied to the altar of sacrifice in the court, the pouring out, the rest poured at the base, the full giving of the life, the figure, the blood the means of atonement, the life poured out, Christ, Isaiah 53:12); the blood on the horns of the brazen altar (as for the ruler, vv.25) — the atonement (the blood applied at the altar of sacrifice); 'pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar' (the rest of the blood poured at the base, the full giving of the life, the figure, the life poured out, Christ pouring out his soul, Isaiah 53:12, the providence, the blood applied and poured out, the atonement for the common man) — Gill.

themes: And the priest shall take of the blood thereof with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar
the blood of the common man's offering on the altar
the atonement applied at the altar of sacrifice, as for the ruler (vv.25)
the life poured out, the figure of Christ pouring out his soul (Isaiah 53:12)

schools.summary: The lamb as an alternative sin offering: 'and if he bring a lamb for a sin offering, he shall bring it a female without blemish' — the common man might bring a female lamb (instead of a goat) for a sin offering (the lamb, the alternative victim, the female lamb, without blemish, the choice, the goat or the lamb, the offerer's provision, the figure, the lamb, the figure of Christ the Lamb of God, John 1:29; 1 Peter 1:19, 'a lamb without blemish and without spot'); the lamb as an alternative (the goat or the lamb, the offerer's choice) — the provision (the way of atonement adaptable to the offerer); 'he shall bring it a female without blemish' (the unblemished female lamb, the spotless victim, the figure, Christ the Lamb without blemish, John 1:29; 1 Peter 1:19, the providence, the lamb sin offering, the figure of the Lamb of God) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And if he bring a lamb for a sin offering, he shall bring it a female without blemish
the lamb as an alternative sin offering
the alternative victim, the unblemished female lamb, the offerer's choice
the figure of Christ the Lamb of God without blemish (John 1:29; 1 Peter 1:19)

schools.summary: The laying on of the hand and the slaying of the lamb: 'and he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay it for a sin offering in the place where they kill the burnt offering' — the offerer laying his hand on the lamb's head and slaying it at the place of sacrifice (the laying on of the hand, the identification and substitution, the sin transferred, as vv.4, the slaying, the death of the substitute, the place, the place of the burnt offering, the figure, the sin laid on the substitute, the death in the sinner's place, fulfilled in Christ, Isaiah 53:6); the hand laid on the lamb (the sin transferred, vv.4) — the figure (the sin laid on Christ the Lamb, Isaiah 53:6; John 1:29); 'slay it for a sin offering in the place where they kill the burnt offering' (the death of the substitute at the place of sacrifice, the figure, the death of Christ the Lamb, Hebrews 9:22; 1 Peter 1:19, the providence, the lamb slain for the sinner, the figure of Christ) — Gill.

themes: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay it for a sin offering in the place where they kill the burnt offering
the laying on of the hand and the slaying of the lamb
the sin transferred to the lamb, which dies in the sinner's place (vv.4)
the figure of Christ the Lamb of God bearing the sin (Isaiah 53:6; John 1:29)

schools.summary: The blood of the lamb offering on the altar: 'and the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar' — the priest putting the blood on the horns of the brazen altar and pouring the rest at its base, as before (vv.30, the blood on the horns, the atonement applied at the altar of sacrifice, the pouring out, the rest poured at the base, the full giving of the life, the figure, the blood the means of atonement, the life poured out, Christ the Lamb, Isaiah 53:12; 1 Peter 1:19); the blood on the horns (the atonement applied, vv.30) — the atonement (the blood at the altar of sacrifice); 'pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar' (the rest of the blood poured, the full giving of the life, the figure, the life poured out, Christ pouring out his soul, Isaiah 53:12, the providence, the blood applied and poured out, the atonement) — JFB, Gill.

themes: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar
the blood of the lamb offering on the altar
the atonement applied at the altar of sacrifice (vv.30)
the life poured out, the figure of Christ the Lamb pouring out his soul (Isaiah 53:12)

schools.summary: The atonement made and the sinner forgiven: 'and he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat of the lamb is taken away from the sacrifice of the peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them upon the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the LORD: and the priest shall make an atonement for his sin that he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him' — the close — 'the priest shall make an atonement for his sin that he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him': the fat burned on the altar (as in the peace offering), and the priest making atonement, and the sin being forgiven (the witnesses noting the significance — the great refrain and promise of the whole chapter, repeated for each rank: 'it shall be forgiven him,' the atonement made by the blood, and the sure forgiveness granted, the lesson — the forgiveness of sins through the atoning sacrifice, the end and gift of the sin offering, the pardon assured, the conscience cleansed, the figure — the forgiveness of sins through the one sacrifice of Christ, Hebrews 9:22, 'without shedding of blood is no remission,' Hebrews 10:18, 'where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin,' Ephesians 1:7; 1 John 1:7-9; Acts 13:38-39, 'through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins,' the assured pardon for every penitent sinner, high and low, through the blood, the close of the chapter on the gracious promise of forgiveness); the fat burned upon the altar, upon the offerings made by fire (the best given to God, as in the peace offering, Leviticus 3:5) — the figure (the sweet savour, the inward devotion of Christ even in the sin offering); the atonement made, the sin forgiven — the assured pardon (the providence, the atonement and forgiveness, the close of the law of the sin offering) — Ellicott, Wesley.

debates.question: What does the closing promise 'it shall be forgiven him' teach?

sides.position: The great refrain and promise of the chapter, the atonement made by the blood and the sure forgiveness granted for each rank (the pardon assured, the conscience cleansed) - a figure of the forgiveness of sins through the one sacrifice of Christ (Hebrews 9:22; 10:18; Ephesians 1:7; Acts 13:38-39), the assured pardon for every penitent sinner through the blood

themes: And the priest shall make an atonement for his sin that he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him
the atonement made and the sinner forgiven (the crux of forgiveness through the sin offering)
the great refrain of the chapter, the atonement by the blood and the assured forgiveness for each rank
a figure of the forgiveness of sins through the one sacrifice of Christ (Hebrews 9:22; 10:18; Ephesians 1:7)
